channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Grizzlies' Jaylen Wells breaks his wrist after hard landing sends rookie to the hospital
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Memphis starting guard Jaylen Wells broke his right wrist on a hard fall on his arm and head from a midair collision at the end of a fast-break dunk during a victory over Charlotte on Tuesday, likely ending the rookie's season right before the Grizzlies go into the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: RICHARD WALKER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jaylon Johnson wants to see real accountability before he says the Bears have changed for the better
item.description: Jaylon Johnson is heading into his sixth NFL season and third go-around riding Chicago's head coaching carousel, and is excited to see the positive change that has eluded them since he joined the franchise, and is looking forward to the Bears' fresh start with new head coach Ben Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Cavaliers claim the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Bulls
item.description: Darius Garland scored 28 points, Evan Mobley had 21 points, 11 rebounds and seven assists and the Cleveland Cavaliers clinched the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 135-113 win over the Chicago Bulls.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulik, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Baseball: Canyon Hills 10, Southwest 7
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Great Monday to play some baseball with Southwest hosting Canyon Hills. Rattlers go on to win a score fest 10-7. The Rattlers scored six in the first and held on to win, despite Southwest cutting it down to 9-7 before tacking on one more run to win by three.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Michael Malone takes the fall despite being the least of the Nuggets' multitude of headaches
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Jamal Murray’s delicate health. Michael Porter Jr.’s dizzying slumps. Aaron Gordon’s balky right calf. Russell Westbrook’s boneheaded plays. An indifferent defense. A paper-thin bench. An ever-changing starting lineup.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Wacko antics between innings
item.description: Racing the mascot around the bases, musical chairs and a wooden horse race have been staples between innings at Excite Ballpark, known until 2019 as Municipal Stadium, or simply “Muni.
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Jon Rahm and Bryson DeChambeau are back in the fold at the Masters. It's a rare occasion
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — For now, there's another tradition unlike any other at the Masters: The first opportunity in nine months for all the world's best players to compete against each other.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Scheffler is back to defend his Masters title, if he can make it through the Champions Dinner safely
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The prerogative of choosing the menu for the Champions Dinner at the Masters falls upon the defending champion, and Scottie Scheffler made an addition in his second go-around at those selections that underscored his wry sense of humor. His dad used to make wonderful meatballs.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Octavio Dotel, who once held record of pitching for 13 major league teams, dies in DR roof collapse
item.description: Former major league relief pitcher Octavio Dotel was among the dead after a roof collapsed at a nightclub in his native Dominican Republic where he was attending a merengue concert.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Detroit Tigers pitcher among at least 44 killed in Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: A former Detroit Tigers pitcher was among at least 44 people killed when the roof of a nightclub collapsed in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Derick Hutchinson, Local 4

item.title: World Series champion Octavio Dotel pulled from rubble after roof collapses at nightclub: report
item.description: Octavio Dotel was born in Santo Domingo and had stints with 13 MLB teams during his 15-year major league career. He last appeared in a MLB game in 2013.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 49ers star’s high school retired his jersey
item.description: (KRON) – For the first time, a Colorado high school has retired a jersey for one of its former students. That alum was none other than San Francisco 49ers star Christian McCaffrey. Valor Christian High School recently retired McCaffrey’s No.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Florida's run to the national title lifts the Gators to No. 1 in the final AP Top 25 men's poll
item.description: Florida is No. 1 in the final Associated Press Top 25 men's college basketball poll of the 2024-25 season after winning the national championship.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 89th Masters Tournament
item.description: After Masters week began with a day of rain on Monday, Augusta National Golf Club was bathed in sunshine for Tuesday's practice round.
item.dc:creator: John Hart, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods teams up with Augusta National to build par-3 course at the Patch
item.description: Tiger Woods and Augusta National announced they have teamed up to build a par-3 course at the facility known as the Patch in Augusta, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr.'s defensive stop gives Florida its 3rd national title with 65-63 win over Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida’s Walter Clayton Jr. came up with the perfect going-away present for that spirit-crushing Houston defense that bullied, battered and bedeviled him all night. It was a defensive gem of his own. Right before the buzzer. For the win and the national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Houston's Kelvin Sampson was close, but 1st national title slipped away painfully vs. Florida
item.description: All Kelvin Sampson could do was stand there, hands on hips with a blank look on his face, as the ball bounced loose and Houston’s latest chance at a national title bounced away, too.
item.dc:creator: By AARON BEARD AP Basketball Writer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houston on the wrong side of gritty and ugly finish against Florida in national title game
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — This ending was gritty and ugly, just the way Houston usually likes games. Except it was Florida forcing bad shots and turnovers against the Cougars to finish off the national championship game Monday night.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Retzlaff poised for another big year at BYU
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Last season, BYU quarterback Jake Retzlaff threw for almost 3,000 yards, 20 touchdowns, while rushing for over 400 yards and six TD's as the Cougars shocked everyone with an 11-2 season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens more tariffs on China as global markets shudder over how much pain economy can take
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Undeterred by a panicked stock market, President Donald Trump threatened additional tariffs on China on Monday, raising fresh concerns that his drive to rebalance the global economy could intensify a financially destructive trade war.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bulls championship banners back up in United Center rafters earlier than expected
item.description: The Chicago Bulls' six championship banners have been rehung at the United Center after being removed due to damage from a rock concert by Disturbed.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Florida makes push all the way to NCAA title game, will face Houston team that pushes others around
item.description: Florida has gone from being picked to finish sixth in the rugged Southeastern Conference to pushing all the way to the final Monday night of the season.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bears' Jaquan Brisker feels 'best I ever felt,' is eager to test Ben Johnson's offense
item.description: Jaquan Brisker is recovering from a concussion and is eager to face Ben Johnson's offense as the Bears begin their offseason workouts, with a focus on exercise and cognitive performance.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sharkey, WGN 9

item.title: LA Dodgers visit White House to celebrate World Series win
item.description: Team president Stan Kasten said all the players wanted to attend, according to the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Women's NCAA Final Four tournament a 'slam dunk for Tampa's economy
item.description: Even though the women’s championship is over, Tampa businesses are calling this weekend a "slam dunk." This is the fourth time the city has hosted the NCAA women’s basketball Final Four, and it’s not just basketball fans who are winning.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Houston rallies to beat Duke 70-67 in the Final Four, will face Florida for the NCAA title
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Sorry guys, “Phi Slama Jama” is already taken. How ’bout something simple, like “Comeback Kids.” “National champs” might be a possibility, too.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Clayton leads Florida to NCAA title game, scoring 34 points in 79-73 victory over SEC rival Auburn
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Walter Clayton Jr. is having an NCAA Tournament for the ages. Florida now just needs him to deliver one more time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Carmelo Anthony and Sue Bird highlight Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Fox 5

item.title: Vucevic, White, Giddey lead the post-season bound Bulls past the Trail Blazers, 118-113
item.description: Nikola Vucevic and Coby White each had 31 points, Josh Giddey had a triple-double and the post-season bound Chicago Bulls beat the Portland Trail Blazers 118-113 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Defending national champion South Carolina returns to women's title game with 74-57 rout of Texas
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Dawn Staley has never lost a national championship game in three trips as South Carolina's coach. Part of the key to winning those decisive games? Forgetting about what happened the last time she was there. And this time, that was only a year ago.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Capitals beat Blackhawks 5-3, Ovechkin scores two goals to tie Gretzky
item.description: Alex Ovechkin tied Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record by scoring the 893rd and 894th of his career, the second being the game winner, as the Washington Capitals rallied to beat the Chicago Blackhawks 5-3 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Imanaga and Happ lead the Cubs past the Padres 3-1 in Wrigley Field opener
item.description: Shota Imanaga pitched one-run ball into the eighth inning in Chicago's home opener at Wrigley Field, and the Cubs handed San Diego its first loss with a 3-1 victory over the Padres on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Doug Bruno 'feels great,' but says stepping aside is the right call as Jill Pizzotti takes over at DePaul
item.description: CHICAGO – Less than a week after Hall of Fame head coach Doug Bruno stepped down for health reasons, the DePaul women’s basketball program named his successor—and it’s a familiar name to fans.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: Two De Beque Dragons sign to college: Ava Vines and Allyson Sandidge
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Despite having a population of less than 500, despite having just a small 1A school, De Beque, Colorado, and the De Beque Dragons have had a lineage of college athletes come through their program.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: BYU guard Dallin Hall enters transfer portal
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU point guard announced on Wednesday that he is entering the transfer portal after three seasons with the Cougars.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: ESPN commentator weighs in on NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: Athletes from four collegiate teams are calling Tampa home for the next few days as the NCAA's Women’s Final Four returns.
item.dc:creator: Nyesha Harper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club brought home Gold in Las Vegas
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Once high school volleyball season ends, several local high school athletes from all across the Western Slope come together to compete at the club level for the Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
